title: Per-line rounding drifts multi-item cart totals by cents
product_summary: Acme billing service (Node/Express): cart pricing, invoicing, payment capture, PSP webhooks

ground_truth.root_cause: computeCartTotalCents() in src/totals.js converts each line item to dollars, applies tax per line, and rounds every line to two decimals with toFixed() before summing — accumulating per-line rounding error, so multi-item carts charge a few cents more or less than the documented subtotal-then-tax rule. The 2026-06 TAX_RATE change in src/config.js is correct and unrelated, despite the stale comment there blaming it for off-by-cents reports.
ground_truth.fix_contract: Totals are computed in integer cents with sales tax applied once to the cart subtotal and a single rounding to the nearest cent at the end: ten 145-cent lines at 7.25% tax total 1555 cents (not 1560), seven 105-cent lines total 788 cents (not 791), and the mixed cart [2x89c, 3x105c, 1x145c] totals 684 cents (not 685). Zero-tax, empty, and single-line cart totals are unchanged.
expected_evidence: payment_disputed events with reason=amount_mismatch concentrated on multi-item orders (item_count > 1)
order_priced totals for multi-item carts deviating from subtotal x 1.0725 by 1-5 cents while single-item carts match exactly
discrepancies predate and postdate the June tax-rate change identically once carts are split by item count
distractors: the 2026-06 TAX_RATE bump in src/config.js — the value is correct, and a stale comment there blames it for off-by-cents reports
PSP-side fees or currency conversion on the payment provider
